family.name: litinski_addsub_schoolbook_v1
family.summary: Exact arithmetic-kernel family with explicit stage/block inventories for schoolbook multiplication, ripple add/sub, conditional select, and fixed multiplication by 21.
family.exact_scope: exact non-Clifford counts and explicit stage/block inventories for the named arithmetic-kernel family; Clifford micro-counts remain outside the shipped lowering layer

source_references.title: Quantum schoolbook multiplication with fewer Toffoli gates
source_references.reason: Provides the controlled add-subtract schoolbook multiplier family and its n^2 + 2n - 1 Toffoli-style cost model.

source_references.title: Halving the cost of quantum addition
source_references.reason: Provides the temporary logical-AND adder family used for the n-1-cost add/sub/select kernels at this layer.

notes: This family lowers the arithmetic side into explicit stage/block inventories instead of treating add/sub/mul/select costs as bare scalar formulas.
The lowering stays at the non-Clifford block layer. It does not yet publish bit-for-bit Clifford micro-expansions for every 256-bit kernel.

kernels.summary: Exact schoolbook controlled add-subtract field-multiplication kernel over the checked 256-bit field width.

stages.name: partial_products

blocks.name: partial_product_grid
blocks.summary: One schoolbook partial-product interaction for each pair of field bits.
notes: This stage records the n^2 schoolbook bit-product interactions in the controlled add-subtract multiplier family.
The partial-product stage is the dominant n^2 contribution in the multiplier family.

stages.name: controlled_add_path
stages.summary: Carry-resolution path for the controlled-add contribution.

blocks.name: controlled_add_accumulator
blocks.summary: Carry-resolution path for the controlled-add half of the schoolbook multiplier.
notes: This stage accounts for the n-1 carry transitions in the add half of the controlled add-subtract multiplier.
The add path follows the same temporary logical-AND interpretation used by the field-adder kernel.

stages.name: controlled_sub_path
stages.summary: Borrow-resolution path for the controlled-subtract contribution.

blocks.name: controlled_sub_accumulator
blocks.summary: Borrow-resolution path for the controlled-subtract half of the schoolbook multiplier.
notes: This stage accounts for the residual n borrow transitions in the subtract half of the controlled add-subtract multiplier.
The subtract path carries the final linear correction term in the Litinski-style controlled add-subtract multiplier.
The kernel reconstructs n^2 + 2n - 1 non-Clifford operations as an explicit partial-product grid plus add/sub correction paths.

kernels.summary: Exact n-bit ripple-carry field-adder kernel over the checked leaf register width.

stages.name: carry_resolution
stages.summary: Temporary logical-AND carry ladder for the field-adder kernel.

blocks.name: temporary_and_carry_ladder
blocks.summary: One temporary logical-AND edge per carry transition in the ripple-carry adder.
notes: This stage follows the temporary logical-AND carry pattern used for n-bit addition.
The local measurement-reset path is counted inside the same stage inventory.
The adder kernel is counted as a single carry-resolution stage because the repository only prices non-Clifford work at this layer.
The kernel contributes n-1 non-Clifford operations for a 256-bit field addition.

kernels.summary: Exact n-bit ripple-carry field-subtractor kernel over the checked leaf register width.

stages.name: borrow_resolution
stages.summary: Temporary logical-AND borrow ladder for the field-subtractor kernel.

blocks.name: temporary_and_borrow_ladder
blocks.summary: One temporary logical-AND edge per borrow transition in the ripple-carry subtractor.
notes: The subtractor reuses the same n-1 temporary logical-AND structure as the adder, interpreted as a borrow ladder.
The subtractor kernel is counted as a single borrow-resolution stage at the non-Clifford layer.
The kernel contributes n-1 non-Clifford operations for a 256-bit field subtraction.

kernels.summary: Exact fixed-constant multiplication kernel for multiplication by 21.

stages.name: addition_chain
stages.summary: Monotone addition-chain realization for multiplication by 21.

blocks.name: chain_step_1_to_2
blocks.summary: One field-add kernel step in the monotone addition chain 1 -> 2.
notes: Each chain step reuses the checked field-add kernel cost over the same 256-bit register width.

blocks.name: chain_step_2_to_4
blocks.summary: One field-add kernel step in the monotone addition chain 2 -> 4.
notes: Each chain step reuses the checked field-add kernel cost over the same 256-bit register width.

blocks.name: chain_step_4_to_8
blocks.summary: One field-add kernel step in the monotone addition chain 4 -> 8.
notes: Each chain step reuses the checked field-add kernel cost over the same 256-bit register width.

blocks.name: chain_step_8_to_16
blocks.summary: One field-add kernel step in the monotone addition chain 8 -> 16.
notes: Each chain step reuses the checked field-add kernel cost over the same 256-bit register width.

blocks.name: chain_step_16_to_20
blocks.summary: One field-add kernel step in the monotone addition chain 16 -> 20.
notes: Each chain step reuses the checked field-add kernel cost over the same 256-bit register width.

blocks.name: chain_step_20_to_21
blocks.summary: One field-add kernel step in the monotone addition chain 20 -> 21.
notes: Each chain step reuses the checked field-add kernel cost over the same 256-bit register width.
The checked leaf uses a fixed 3b = 21 multiplier, so the addition chain is exact and machine-readable.
The kernel uses the exact monotone addition chain [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 20, 21] for multiplication by 21.

kernels.summary: Exact field-width conditional-select kernel used by the neutral-entry bypass path.

stages.name: controlled_move
stages.summary: Bitwise conditional move under the one-bit lookup-infinity flag.

blocks.name: bitwise_control_ladder
blocks.summary: One controlled field-bit select for each nontrivial bit position in the destination register.
notes: The select kernel is treated as a field-width controlled move whose non-Clifford cost matches the field-add kernel at this abstraction layer.
The controlled move stays within the checked leaf register file and does not introduce a separate lookup-family dependency.
The kernel contributes n-1 non-Clifford operations for a 256-bit conditional field select.
